nodes.name: Ask user's first and last name

transition_condition.prompt: Received the user's full name (first and last) — either confirmed or clearly understood
User declines to give any either the first name or last name

instruction.text:
Ask the user for their full name — first and last name.

Say something like: "Could I get your full name, please?"

If the user only gives a first name (e.g. "Bob"), ask for the last name too: "And your last name, Bob?"

If the user only gives a last name or it's unclear which is which, clarify: "Just to be sure — what's your first name?"

Confirmation — only when needed:
- If the name is common and clearly heard (e.g. "Bob Jones", "Maria Garcia"), just acknowledge it and move on: 
  "Great, thanks Bob!"
- If the name is unusual, has an ambiguous spelling, or the user seemed to struggle saying it, read it back: "Just to make sure I have it right — that's [First] [Last]?" - If the user spells it out or corrects you, always confirm the corrected version: "Got it — [corrected name]."

If the user gives a middle name as well, acknowledge it but focus on first and last: "Thanks! I'll have you down as 
[First] [Last]."

If the user refuses to provide their name: Explain why it's needed: "I completely understand — I only ask because the clinic needs your name to set up the appointment and pull 
up your records. It's kept totally confidential."

Do NOT ask more than twice.

transition_condition.prompt: first name and last name received
User wants to try again or provide more information
User is done and wants to end the call

instruction.text:
The user was unable or unwilling to provide their first name or last name
 
Politely let them know that you may need this information to complete the booking, and offer alternatives:
- They can call back when they have the information ready

Ask if there's anything else you can help with.If they still decline after being reassured, let them know that's okay and move on. Do not ask more than two times total for any piece of information.
